or --help/-h\\\\\\\\n    let args: Vec&lt;String&gt; = std::env::args().collect();\\\\\\\\n    let should_show_splash =\\\\\\\\n        args.len() == 1 || (args.len() == 2 &amp;&amp; (args[1] == \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"--help\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" || args[1] == \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"-h\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"));\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    if should_show_splash {\\\\\\\\n        print_splash_screen();\\\\\\\\n tracing_subscriber::fmt()\\\\\\\\n            .with_max_level(tracing::Level::DEBUG)\\\\\\\\n            .init();\\\\\\\\n    } else if cli.quiet {\\\\\\\\n        tracing_subscriber::fmt()\\\\\\\\n .with_max_level(tracing::Level::INFO)\\\\\\\\n            .init();\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    // Track command start\\\\\\\\n    let command_name = format!(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"{:?}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", cli.command);\\\\\\\\n    let args: Vec&lt;String&gt; = std::env::args().collect();\\\\\\\\n    telemetry.track_command_start(&amp;command_name, &amp;args);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    // Handle dry run mode\\\\\\\\n    if cli.dry_run {\\\\\\\\n        let output = cli.output;\\\\\\\\n        match handle_dry_run(&amp;cli.command, output).await }\\\\\\\\n            Err(e) =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n                let boxed_error: Box&lt;dyn std::error::Error&gt; = Box::new(e);\\\\\\\\n                print_error(&amp;boxed_error, output);\\\\\\\\n let output = if cli.quiet {\\\\\\\\n        OutputFormat::Quiet\\\\\\\\n    } else if cli.openclaw {\\\\\\\\n        OutputFormat::Json\\\\\\\\n    } else {\\\\\\\\n        cli.output\\\\\\\\n    };\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    // Execute command and handle errors\\\\\\\\n    match execute_command(cli.command, output, cli.verbose).await {\\\\\\\\n        Ok(_) =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n            telemetry.track_command_complete(&amp;command_name, true, None);\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n        Err(e) =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n            print_error(&amp;e, output);\\\\\\\\n the command, converting anyhow errors to our Error type\\\\\\\\nasync fn execute_command(\\\\\\\\n    command: Commands,\\\\\\\\n    output: OutputFormat,\\\\\\\\n    verbose: bool,\\\\\\\\n) -&gt; Result&lt;(), Box&lt;dyn std::error::Error&gt;&gt; {\\\\\\\\n    match command {\\\\\\\\n        Commands::Config { command } =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n            commands::handle_config(command, {\\\\\\\\n            commands::handle_leverage(command, output).await?;\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n        Commands::Margin { command } =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n            commands::handle_margin(command).await?;\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n        Commands::Stream { command } =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n            commands::handle_stream(command, verbose).await?;\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n        Commands::Dashboard {\\\\\\\\n            symbols,\\\\\\\\n            verbose,\\\\\\\\n            watch,\\\\\\\\n            compact,\\\\\\\\n        } =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n            let command = DashboardCommands::Snapshot {\\\\\\\\n                symbols,\\\\\\\\n                verbose,\\\\\\\\n                watch,\\\\\\\\n                compact,\\\\\\\\n            };\\\\\\\\n            commands::handle_dashboard(command, output).await?;\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n        Commands::Portfolio { verbose, watch } =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n            let command = crate::commands::PortfolioCommand::Snapshot { _verbose: verbose, watch };\\\\\\\\n            commands::handle_portfolio(command, output).await?;\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n    Ok(())\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/// Handle dry run mode - show what would be executed\\\\\\\\nasync fn handle_dry_run(command: &amp;Commands, output: OutputFormat) -&gt; Result&lt;(), standx_cli::Error&gt; {\\\\\\\\n    let description = match command {\\\\\\\\n        Commands::Config { .. } =&gt; \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Would modify configuration\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\n        Commands::Auth { .. } =&gt; \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Would authenticate with StandX\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\n        Commands::Market { .. } =&gt; \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Would fetch market data (read-only, safe to execute)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\n        Commands::Account { .. } =&gt; \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Would fetch account data (read-only, safe to execute)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\n        Commands::Order { .. } =&gt; \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"⚠️  WOULD CREATE/CANCEL ORDER - FINANCIAL IMPACT\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\n        Commands::Trade { .. } =&gt; \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Would fetch trade history (read-only, safe to execute)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\n        Commands::Leverage { .. } =&gt; \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"⚠️  WOULD MODIFY LEVERAGE - POSITION IMPACT\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\n        Commands::Margin { .. } =&gt; \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"⚠️  WOULD MODIFY MARGIN - POSITION IMPACT\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\n        Commands::Stream { .. } =&gt; \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Would start real-time data stream\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\n        Commands::Dashboard { .. } =&gt; \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Would fetch dashboard data (read-only, safe to execute)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\n        Commands::Portfolio { .. } =&gt; \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Would fetch portfolio data (read-only, safe to execute)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\n    };\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    let dry_run_info = serde_json::json!({\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"dry_run\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": true,\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"command\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": format!(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"{:?}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", command),\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"description\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": description,\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"would_execute\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": !matches!(command, Commands::Order { .. } | Commands::Leverage { .. } | Commands::Margin { .. }),\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"note\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Remove --dry-run to execute\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n    });\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    match output {\\\\\\\\n        OutputFormat::Json =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n            println!(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"{}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&amp;dry_run_info).unwrap());\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n        _ =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n            println!(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"🔍 DRY RUN MODE\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\");\\\\\\\\n            println!(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"{}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", dry_run_info[\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"description\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"].as_str().unwrap());\\\\\\\\n            if !dry_run_info[\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"would_execute\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"].as_bool().unwrap() {\\\\\\\\n                println!(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"⚠️  This command would have financial impact\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\");\\\\\\\\n            }\\\\\\\\n            println!(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nRemove --dry-run to let command = DashboardCommands::Snapshot {\\\\\\\\n                symbols,\\\\\\\\n                verbose,\\\\\\\\n                watch,\\\\\\\\n                compact,\\\\\\\\n Commands::Auth { .. } =&gt; \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Would authenticate with StandX\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\n        Commands::Market { .. } =&gt; \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Would fetch market data (read-only, safe to execute)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\n        Commands::Account { .. } =&gt; \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Would fetch account data (read-only, safe to execute)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\n        Commands::Order { .. } =&gt; \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"⚠️  WOULD CREATE/CANCEL ORDER - FINANCIAL IMPACT\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\n        Commands::Trade { .. } =&gt; \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Would fetch trade history (read-only, safe to execute)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\n        Commands::Leverage { .. } =&gt; \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"⚠️  WOULD MODIFY LEVERAGE - POSITION IMPACT\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\n        Commands::Margin { .. } =&gt; fetch portfolio data (read-only, safe to execute)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\n    };\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    let dry_run_info = serde_json::json!({\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"dry_run\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": true,\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"command\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": format!(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"{:?}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", command),\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"description\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": description,\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"would_execute\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": !matches!(command, Commands::Order { .. } | Commands::Leverage { .. } | Commands::Margin { .. }),\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"note\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Remove --dry-run to execute\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n    });\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    match output {\\\\\\\\n        OutputFormat::Json =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n            println!(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"{}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&amp;dry_run_info).unwrap());\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n        _ =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n            println!(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"🔍 DRY RUN MODE\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\");\\\\\\\\n            println!(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"{}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", Order API client methods\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nuse crate::client::StandXClient;\\\\\\\\nuse crate::error::{Error, Result};\\\\\\\\nuse crate::models::{Order, OrderSide, OrderType, TimeInForce};\\\\\\\\nuse serde_json::json;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/// Order request parameters\\\\\\\\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\\\\\\\\npub struct CreateOrderParams {\\\\\\\\n    pub symbol: String,\\\\\\\\n    pub side: OrderSide,\\\\\\\\n    pub order_type: OrderType,\\\\\\\\n    pub quantity: String,\\\\\\\\n    pub price: Option&lt;String&gt;,\\\\\\\\n    pub time_in_force: Option&lt;TimeInForce&gt;,\\\\\\\\n    pub reduce_only: bool,\\\\\\\\n    pub stop_price: Option&lt;String&gt;,\\\\\\\\n    pub sl_price: Option&lt;String&gt;,\\\\\\\\n    pub tp_price: Option&lt;String&gt;,\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nimpl Default for CreateOrderParams {\\\\\\\\n    fn default() -&gt; Self {\\\\\\\\n        Self {\\\\\\\\n            symbol: String::new(),\\\\\\\\n            side: OrderSide::Buy,\\\\\\\\n            order_type: OrderType::Limit,\\\\\\\\n            quantity: String::new(),\\\\\\\\n            price: None,\\\\\\\\n            time_in_force: None,\\\\\\\\n            reduce_only: false,\\\\\\\\n            stop_price: None,\\\\\\\\n            sl_price: None,\\\\\\\\n            tp_price: None,\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/// Order API methods\\\\\\\\nimpl StandXClient {\\\\\\\\n    /// Create a new order\\\\\\\\n    pub async fn create_order(&amp;self, params: CreateOrderParams) -&gt; Result&lt;Order&gt; {\\\\\\\\n        let url = format!(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"{}/api/new_order\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", self.base_url);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        // Build request body\\\\\\\\n        let order_type = match params.order_type {\\\\\\\\n            OrderType::Market =&gt; \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"market\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\n            OrderType::Limit =&gt; \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"limit\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\n        };\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        let side = match params.side {\\\\\\\\n            OrderSide::Buy =&gt; \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"buy\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\n            OrderSide::Sell =&gt; \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"sell\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\n        };\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        let tif = params\\\\\\\\n            .time_in_force\\\\\\\\n            .map(|t| match t {\\\\\\\\n                TimeInForce::Gtc =&gt; \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"gtc\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\n                TimeInForce::Ioc =&gt; \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"ioc\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\n                TimeInForce::Fok =&gt; \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"fok\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\n            })\\\\\\\\n            .unwrap_or(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"gtc\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\");\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        let mut body = json!({\\\\\\\\n            \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"symbol\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": params.symbol,\\\\\\\\n            \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"side\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": side,\\\\\\\\n            \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"order_type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": order_type,\\\\\\\\n            \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"qty\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": params.quantity,\\\\\\\\n            \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"time_in_force\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": tif,\\\\\\\\n            \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"reduce_only\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": params.reduce_only,\\\\\\\\n        });\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        // Add optional fields\\\\\\\\n        if let Some(ref price) = params.price {\\\\\\\\n            body[\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"price\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"] = json!(price);\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n        if let Some(stop_price) = params.stop_price {\\\\\\\\n            body[\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"stop_price\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"] = json!(stop_price);\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n        if let Some(sl_price) = params.sl_price {\\\\\\\\n            body[\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"sl_price\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"] = json!(sl_price);\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n        if let Some(tp_price) = params.tp_price {\\\\\\\\n            body[\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"tp_price\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"] = json!(tp_price);\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        let body_str = body.to_string();\\\\\\\\n        let headers = self.build_auth_headers(Some(&amp;body_str)).await?;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        let response = self\\\\\\\\n            .client\\\\\\\\n            .post(&amp;url)\\\\\\\\n            .headers(headers)\\\\\\\\n            .body(body_str)\\\\\\\\n            .send()\\\\\\\\n            .await?;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        if !response.status().is_success() {\\\\\\\\n            let status = response.status();\\\\\\\\n            let text = response.text().await.unwrap_or_default();\\\\\\\\n            return Err(Error::Api {\\\\\\\\n                code: status.as_u16(),\\\\\\\\n                message: text,\\\\\\\\n                endpoint: Some(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"/api/new_order\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\".to_string()),\\\\\\\\n                retryable: status.as_u16() &gt;= 500,\\\\\\\\n            });\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        let result: serde_json::Value = response.json().await?;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        // Check for API error\\\\\\\\n        if let Some(code) = result.get(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"code\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\").and_then(|c| c.as_i64()) {\\\\\\\\n            if code != 0 {\\\\\\\\n                let message = result\\\\\\\\n                    .get(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"message\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\n                    .and_then(|m| m.as_str())\\\\\\\\n                    .unwrap_or(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Order rejected\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\");\\\\\\\\n                return Err(Error::Api {\\\\\\\\n                    code: code as u16,\\\\\\\\n                    message: message.to_string(),\\\\\\\\n                    endpoint: Some(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"/api/new_order\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\".to_string()),\\\\\\\\n                    retryable: false,\\\\\\\\n                });\\\\\\\\n            }\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        // Build order from response\\\\\\\\n        let now = chrono::Utc::now().to_rfc3339();\\\\\\\\n        let order = Order {\\\\\\\\n            id: result\\\\\\\\n                .get(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"request_id\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\n                .and_then(|r| r.as_str())\\\\\\\\n                .unwrap_or(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\n                .to_string(),\\\\\\\\n            symbol: params.symbol,\\\\\\\\n            side: params.side,\\\\\\\\n            order_type: params.order_type,\\\\\\\\n            qty: params.quantity,\\\\\\\\n            fill_qty: \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"0\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\".to_string(),\\\\\\\\n            price: params.price.clone().unwrap_or_else(|| \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"0\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\".to_string()),\\\\\\\\n            status: crate::models::OrderStatus::New,\\\\\\\\n            created_at: now.clone(),\\\\\\\\n            updated_at: now,\\\\\\\\n        };\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        Ok(order)\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    /// Cancel an order by ID\\\\\\\\n    pub async fn cancel_order(&amp;self, symbol: &amp;str, order_id: &amp;str) -&gt; Result&lt;()&gt; {\\\\\\\\n        let url = format!(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"{}/api/cancel_order\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", self.base_url);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        let body = json!({\\\\\\\\n            \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"symbol\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": symbol,\\\\\\\\n            \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"order_id\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": order_id.parse::&lt;i64&gt;().unwrap_or(0),\\\\\\\\n        });\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        let body_str = body.to_string();\\\\\\\\n        let headers = self.build_auth_headers(Some(&amp;body_str)).await?;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        let response = self\\\\\\\\n            .client\\\\\\\\n            .post(&amp;url)\\\\\\\\n            .headers(headers)\\\\\\\\n            .body(body_str)\\\\\\\\n            .send()\\\\\\\\n            .await?;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        if !response.status().is_success() {\\\\\\\\n            let status = response.status();\\\\\\\\n            let text = response.text().await.unwrap_or_default();\\\\\\\\n            return Err(Error::Api {\\\\\\\\n                code: status.as_u16(),\\\\\\\\n                message: text,\\\\\\\\n                endpoint: Some(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"/api/cancel_order\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\".to_string()),\\\\\\\\n                retryable: status.as_u16() &gt;= 500,\\\\\\\\n            });\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        Ok(())\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    /// Cancel all orders for a symbol\\\\\\\\n    pub async fn cancel_all_orders(&amp;self, symbol: &amp;str) -&gt; Result&lt;()&gt; {\\\\\\\\n        let url = format!(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"{}/api/cancel_orders\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", self.base_url);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        let body = json!({\\\\\\\\n            \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"symbol\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": symbol,\\\\\\\\n        });\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        let body_str = body.to_string();\\\\\\\\n        let headers = self.build_auth_headers(Some(&amp;body_str)).await?;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        let response = self\\\\\\\\n            .client\\\\\\\\n            .post(&amp;url)\\\\\\\\n            .headers(headers)\\\\\\\\n            .body(body_str)\\\\\\\\n            .send()\\\\\\\\n            .await?;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        if !response.status().is_success() {\\\\\\\\n            let status = response.status();\\\\\\\\n            let text = response.text().await.unwrap_or_default();\\\\\\\\n            return Err(Error::Api {\\\\\\\\n                code: status.as_u16(),\\\\\\\\n                message: text,\\\\\\\\n                endpoint: Some(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"/api/cancel_orders\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\".to_string()),\\\\\\\\n                retryable: status.as_u16() &gt;= 500,\\\\\\\\n            });\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        Ok(())\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n#[cfg(test)]\\\\\\\\nmod tests {\\\\\\\\n    use StandXClient {\\\\\\\\n    /// Create a new order\\\\\\\\n    pub async fn create_order(&amp;self, params: CreateOrderParams) -&gt; Result&lt;Order&gt; {\\\\\\\\n        let url = format!(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"{}/api/new_order\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", self.base_url);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        // Build request body\\\\\\\\n        let order_type = match params.order_type {\\\\\\\\n            OrderType::Market =&gt; \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"market\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\n            OrderType::Limit =&gt; \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"limit\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\n        };\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        let side = match params.side {\\\\\\\\n            OrderSide::Buy =&gt; \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"buy\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\n            OrderSide::Sell =&gt; \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"sell\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\n        };\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        let tif = params\\\\\\\\n            .time_in_force\\\\\\\\n            .map(|t| match t {\\\\\\\\n                TimeInForce::Gtc =&gt; \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"gtc\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\n                TimeInForce::Ioc =&gt; \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"ioc\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\n                TimeInForce::Fok =&gt; \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"fok\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\n            })\\\\\\\\n            .unwrap_or(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"gtc\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\");\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        let mut body = json!({\\\\\\\\n            \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"symbol\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": params.symbol,\\\\\\\\n            \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"side\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": side,\\\\\\\\n            \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"order_type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": order_type,\\\\\\\\n            \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"qty\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": params.quantity,\\\\\\\\n            \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"time_in_force\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": tif,\\\\\\\\n            \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"reduce_only\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": params.reduce_only,\\\\\\\\n        });\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        // Add optional fields\\\\\\\\n        if let Some(ref price) = params.price {\\\\\\\\n            body[\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"price\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"] = json!(price);\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n        if let Some(stop_price) = params.stop_price {\\\\\\\\n            body[\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"stop_price\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"] = json!(stop_price);\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n        if let Some(sl_price) = params.sl_price {\\\\\\\\n            body[\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"sl_price\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"] = json!(sl_price);\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n        if let Some(tp_price) = params.tp_price {\\\\\\\\n            body[\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"tp_price\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"] = json!(tp_price);\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        let body_str = body.to_string();\\\\\\\\n        let headers = self.build_auth_headers(Some(&amp;body_str)).await?;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        let response = self\\\\\\\\n            .client\\\\\\\\n            .post(&amp;url)\\\\\\\\n            .headers(headers)\\\\\\\\n            .body(body_str)\\\\\\\\n            .send()\\\\\\\\n            .await?;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        if !response.status().is_success() {\\\\\\\\n            let status = response.status();\\\\\\\\n            let text = response.text().await.unwrap_or_default();\\\\\\\\n            return Err(Error::Api {\\\\\\\\n                code: status.as_u16(),\\\\\\\\n                message: text,\\\\\\\\n                endpoint: Some(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"/api/new_order\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\".to_string()),\\\\\\\\n                retryable: status.as_u16() &gt;= 500,\\\\\\\\n            });\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        let result: serde_json::Value = response.json().await?;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        // Check for API error\\\\\\\\n        if let Some(code) = result.get(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"code\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\").and_then(|c| c.as_i64()) {\\\\\\\\n            if code != 0 {\\\\\\\\n                let message = result\\\\\\\\n                    .get(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"message\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\n                    .and_then(|m| m.as_str())\\\\\\\\n                    .unwrap_or(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Order rejected\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\");\\\\\\\\n                return Err(Error::Api {\\\\\\\\n                    code: code as u16,\\\\\\\\n                    message: message.to_string(),\\\\\\\\n                    endpoint: Some(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"/api/new_order\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\".to_string()),\\\\\\\\n                    retryable: false,\\\\\\\\n                });\\\\\\\\n            }\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        // Build order from response\\\\\\\\n        let now = chrono::Utc::now().to_rfc3339();\\\\\\\\n        let order = Order {\\\\\\\\n            id: result\\\\\\\\n                .get(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"request_id\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\n                .and_then(|r| r.as_str())\\\\\\\\n                .unwrap_or(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\n                .to_string(),\\\\\\\\n            symbol: params.symbol,\\\\\\\\n            side: params.side,\\\\\\\\n            order_type: params.order_type,\\\\\\\\n            qty: params.quantity,\\\\\\\\n            fill_qty: \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"0\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\".to_string(),\\\\\\\\n            price: params.price.clone().unwrap_or_else(|| \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"0\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\".to_string()),\\\\\\\\n            status: crate::models::OrderStatus::New,\\\\\\\\n            created_at: now.clone(),\\\\\\\\n            updated_at: now,\\\\\\\\n        };\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        Ok(order)\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    /// Cancel an order by ID\\\\\\\\n    pub async fn cancel_order(&amp;self, symbol: &amp;str, order_id: &amp;str) -&gt; Result&lt;()&gt; {\\\\\\\\n        let url = format!(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"{}/api/cancel_order\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", self.base_url);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        let body = json!({\\\\\\\\n            \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"symbol\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": symbol,\\\\\\\\n Authentication module for StandX API\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\npub mod credentials;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nuse base64::{engine::general_purpose::STANDARD, Engine as _};\\\\\\\\nuse ed25519_dalek::{Signer, SigningKey, VerifyingKey};\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\npub use credentials::Credentials;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/// StandX request signer using Ed25519\\\\\\\\n#[derive(Debug)]\\\\\\\\npub struct StandXSigner {\\\\\\\\n    signing_key: SigningKey,\\\\\\\\n    verifying_key: VerifyingKey,\\\\\\\\n    request_id: String,\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/// Request signature headers\\\\\\\\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\\\\\\\\npub struct RequestSignature {\\\\\\\\n    pub version: String,\\\\\\\\n    pub request_id: String,\\\\\\\\n    pub timestamp: u64,\\\\\\\\n    pub signature: String,\\\\\\\\n    pub pubkey: String,\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nimpl StandXSigner {\\\\\\\\n    /// Create a new signer from a Base58-encoded private key\\\\\\\\n    pub fn from_base58(private_key_base58: &amp;str) -&gt; crate::Result&lt;Self&gt; {\\\\\\\\n        let private_key_bytes = bs58::decode(private_key_base58).into_vec().map_err(|_e| {\\\\\\\\n            crate::Error::InvalidCredentials {\\\\\\\\n                message: \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Invalid private key format\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\".to_string(),\\\\\\\\n            }\\\\\\\\n        })?;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        let signing_key = SigningKey::from_bytes(&amp;private_key_bytes.try_into().map_err(|_| {\\\\\\\\n            crate::Error::InvalidCredentials {\\\\\\\\n                message: \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Invalid private key length\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\".to_string(),\\\\\\\\n            }\\\\\\\\n        })?);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        let verifying_key = signing_key.verifying_key();\\\\\\\\n        let request_id = hex::encode(verifying_key.as_bytes());\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        Ok(Self {\\\\\\\\n            signing_key,\\\\\\\\n            verifying_key,\\\\\\\\n            request_id,\\\\\\\\n        })\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    /// Get the request ID (hex-encoded public key)\\\\\\\\n    pub fn request_id(&amp;self) -&gt; &amp;str {\\\\\\\\n        &amp;self.request_id\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    /// Get the public key in hex format\\\\\\\\n    pub fn pubkey_hex(&amp;self) -&gt; String {\\\\\\\\n        hex::encode(self.verifying_key.as_bytes())\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    /// Sign a request\\\\\\\\n    pub fn sign_request(&amp;self, timestamp: u64, payload: &amp;str) -&gt; RequestSignature {\\\\\\\\n        let version = \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"v1\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\";\\\\\\\\n        let message = format!(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"{},{},{},{}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", version, self.request_id, timestamp, payload);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        let signature = self.signing_key.sign(message.as_bytes());\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        RequestSignature {\\\\\\\\n            version: version.to_string(),\\\\\\\\n            request_id: self.request_id.clone(),\\\\\\\\n            timestamp,\\\\\\\\n            signature: STANDARD.encode(signature.to_bytes()),\\\\\\\\n            pubkey: self.pubkey_hex(),\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    /// Sign a request with the current timestamp\\\\\\\\n    pub fn sign_request_now(&amp;self, payload: &amp;str) -&gt; RequestSignature {\\\\\\\\n        let timestamp = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis() as u64;\\\\\\\\n        self.sign_request(timestamp, payload)\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n#[cfg(test)]\\\\\\\\nmod tests {\\\\\\\\n    use super::*;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    // Test private key (Base58) - DO NOT USE IN PRODUCTION\\\\\\\\n    const TEST_PRIVATE_KEY: &amp;str = \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"HdsyJD7oWgT7tRZ7L8QJ9K3mP5nQ8rS2vX4wY6zA1bC3dE5fG7hI9jK0lM2nO4pQ6rS8tU0vW1xY2zA3bC4dE5fG6h\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\";\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    #[test]\\\\\\\\n    fn test_signer_from_base58() {\\\\\\\\n        // Generate a random key for testing\\\\\\\\n        let signing_key = SigningKey::generate(&amp;mut rand::thread_rng());\\\\\\\\n        let private_key_bytes = signing_key.to_bytes();\\\\\\\\n        let private_key_base58 = bs58::encode(&amp;private_key_bytes).into_string();\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        let signer = StandXSigner::from_base58(&amp;private_key_base58).unwrap();\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        assert!(!signer.request_id().is_empty());\\\\\\\\n        assert!(!signer.pubkey_hex().is_empty());\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    #[test]\\\\\\\\n    fn test_sign_request() {\\\\\\\\n        let signing_key = SigningKey::generate(&amp;mut rand::thread_rng());\\\\\\\\n        let private_key_bytes = signing_key.to_bytes();\\\\\\\\n        let private_key_base58 = bs58::encode(&amp;private_key_bytes).into_string();\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        let signer = StandXSigner::from_base58(&amp;private_key_base58).unwrap();\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        let payload = r#\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"symbol\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"BTC-USD\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"side\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"buy\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"#;\\\\\\\\n        let sig = signer.sign_request(1700000000000, payload);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        assert_eq!(sig.version, \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"v1\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\");\\\\\\\\n        assert_eq!(sig.timestamp, 1700000000000);\\\\\\\\n        assert!(!sig.signature.is_empty());\\\\\\\\n        assert!(!sig.pubkey.is_empty());\\\\\\\\n        assert_eq!(sig.request_id, signer.request_id());\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    #[test]\\\\\\\\n    fn test_invalid_base58() {\\\\\\\\n        let result = StandXSigner::from_base58(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"invalid!!!\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\");\\\\\\\\n        assert!(result.is_err());\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    #[test]\\\\\\\\n    fn test_signature_format() {\\\\\\\\n        let signing_key = SigningKey::generate(&amp;mut rand::thread_rng());\\\\\\\\n        let private_key_bytes = signing_key.to_bytes();\\\\\\\\n        let private_key_base58 = bs58::encode(&amp;private_key_bytes).into_string();\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        let signer = StandXSigner::from_base58(&amp;private_key_base58).unwrap();\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        let payload = r#\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"symbol\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"BTC-USD\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"#;\\\\\\\\n        let sig = signer.sign_request_now(payload);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        // Verify signature is valid base64\\\\\\\\n        let decoded = STANDARD.decode(&amp;sig.signature);\\\\\\\\n        assert!(decoded.is_ok());\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        // Ed25519 signatures are 64 bytes\\\\\\\\n        assert_eq!(decoded.unwrap().len(), 64);\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    #[test]\\\\\\\\n    fn test_signature_verification() {\\\\\\\\n        // Test that signature format is correct and can be decoded\\\\\\\\n        let signing_key = SigningKey::generate(&amp;mut rand::thread_rng());\\\\\\\\n        let private_key_bytes = signing_key.to_bytes();\\\\\\\\n        let private_key_base58 = bs58::encode(&amp;private_key_bytes).into_string();\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        let signer = StandXSigner::from_base58(&amp;private_key_base58).unwrap();\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        let payload = r#\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"symbol\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"BTC-USD\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"side\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"buy\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"#;\\\\\\\\n        let sig = signer.sign_request_now(payload);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        // Decode the signature from base64\\\\\\\\n        let sig_bytes = STANDARD.decode(&amp;sig.signature).unwrap();\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        // Decode the public key from hex\\\\\\\\n        let pubkey_bytes = hex::decode(&amp;sig.pubkey).unwrap();\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        // Verify the signature format is correct\\\\\\\\n        // Ed25519 signatures are 64 bytes\\\\\\\\n        assert_eq!(sig_bytes.len(), 64);\\\\\\\\n        // Ed25519 public keys are 32 bytes\\\\\\\\n        assert_eq!(pubkey_bytes.len(), 32);\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    #[test]\\\\\\\\n    fn test_sign_request_consistency() {\\\\\\\\n        // Test that signing the same payload produces consistent results\\\\\\\\n        // Ed25519 is deterministic - same message + same key = same signature\\\\\\\\n        let signing_key = SigningKey::generate(&amp;mut rand::thread_rng());\\\\\\\\n        let private_key_bytes = signing_key.to_bytes();\\\\\\\\n        let private_key_base58 = bs58::encode(&amp;private_key_bytes).into_string();\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        let signer = StandXSigner::from_base58(&amp;private_key_base58).unwrap();\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        let payload = r#\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"symbol\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"BTC-USD\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"side\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"buy\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"#;\\\\\\\\n        let timestamp = 1700000000000u64;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        // Sign same payload twice\\\\\\\\n        let sig1 = signer.sign_request(timestamp, payload);\\\\\\\\n        let sig2 = signer.sign_request(timestamp, payload);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        // Same signer should produce same request_id and pubkey\\\\\\\\n        assert_eq!(sig1.request_id, sig2.request_id);\\\\\\\\n        assert_eq!(sig1.pubkey, sig2.pubkey);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        // Ed25519 is deterministic, so signatures should be identical\\\\\\\\n        assert_eq!(sig1.signature, sig2.signature);\\\\\\\\n Error::TokenExpired { .. } =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n                Some(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Re-authenticate with 'standx auth login'\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\".to_string())\\\\\\\\n            }\\\\\\\\n            Error::RateLimitExceeded { retry_after, .. } =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n                retry_after.map(|secs| format!(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Wait {} seconds before retrying\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", secs))\\\\\\\\n            }\\\\\\\\n            Error::InvalidSymbol { .. } =&gt; {\\\\\\\\n                Some(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Run 'standx market symbols' to see available symbols\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\".to_string())\\\\\\\\n fn from(e: reqwest::Error) -&gt; Self {\\\\\\\\n        let code = e.status().map(|s| s.as_u16()).unwrap_or(0);\\\\\\\\n        let retryable = code == 0 || code &gt;= 500;\\\\\\\\n        Error::Http {\\\\\\\\n            code,\\\\\\\\n            message: e.to_string(),\\\\\\\\n            retryable: Some(retryable),\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nimpl Telemetry module for collecting usage statistics\\\\\\\\n//!\\\\\\\\n//! This module provides local telemetry collection for standx-cli.\\\\\\\\n//! Data is stored locally and never sent to remote servers.\\\\\\\\n//!\\\\\\\\n//! To disable telemetry, set environment variable: STANDX_TELEMETRY=0\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\\\\\\\\nuse std::fs::{create_dir_all, OpenOptions};\\\\\\\\nuse std::io::Write;\\\\\\\\nuse std::path::PathBuf;\\\\\\\\nuse std::time::Instant;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/// Telemetry configuration\\\\\\\\nconst TELEMETRY_ENV_VAR: &amp;str = \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"STANDX_TELEMETRY\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\";\\\\\\\\nconst TELEMETRY_DISABLED: &amp;str = \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"0\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\";\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/// Telemetry event types\\\\\\\\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\\\\\\\n#[serde(rename_all = \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"snake_case\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")]\\\\\\\\npub enum EventType {\\\\\\\\n    /// Command started execution\\\\\\\\n    CommandStarted,\\\\\\\\n    /// Command completed execution\\\\\\\\n    CommandCompleted,\\\\\\\\n    /// Error occurred\\\\\\\\n    Error,\\\\\\\\n    /// API call made\\\\\\\\n    ApiCall,\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/// Telemetry event\\\\\\\\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\\\\\\\npub struct TelemetryEvent {\\\\\\\\n    /// ISO 8601 timestamp\\\\\\\\n    pub timestamp: String,\\\\\\\\n    /// Event type\\\\\\\\n    pub event_type: EventType,\\\\\\\\n    /// CLI version\\\\\\\\n    pub version: String,\\\\\\\\n    /// Event properties\\\\\\\\n    pub properties: serde_json::Value,\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/// Telemetry collector\\\\\\\\npub struct Telemetry {\\\\\\\\n    enabled: bool,\\\\\\\\n    log_path: PathBuf,\\\\\\\\n    session_id: String,\\\\\\\\n    command_start: Option&lt;Instant&gt;,\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n#[allow(dead_code)]\\\\\\\\nimpl Telemetry {\\\\\\\\n    /// Create new telemetry instance\\\\\\\\n    pub fn new() -&gt; Self {\\\\\\\\n        let enabled = std::env::var(TELEMETRY_ENV_VAR).unwrap_or_default() != TELEMETRY_DISABLED;\\\\\\\\n        let log_path = Self::get_log_path();\\\\\\\\n        let session_id = uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string();\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        // Ensure directory exists\\\\\\\\n        if let Some(parent) = log_path.parent() {\\\\\\\\n            let _ = create_dir_all(parent);\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        let telemetry = Self {\\\\\\\\n            enabled,\\\\\\\\n            log_path,\\\\\\\\n            session_id: session_id.clone(),\\\\\\\\n            command_start: None,\\\\\\\\n        };\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        // Track session start\\\\\\\\n        telemetry.track_event(\\\\\\\\n            EventType::CommandStarted,\\\\\\\\n            serde_json::json!({\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"session_id\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": &amp;session_id,\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"os\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": std::env::consts::OS,\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"arch\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": std::env::consts::ARCH,\\\\\\\\n            }),\\\\\\\\n        );\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        telemetry\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    /// Get telemetry log file path\\\\\\\\n    fn get_log_path() -&gt; PathBuf {\\\\\\\\n        dirs::data_dir()\\\\\\\\n            .unwrap_or_else(|| PathBuf::from(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\".\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"))\\\\\\\\n            .join(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"standx\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\n            .join(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"telemetry.log\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    /// Check if telemetry is enabled\\\\\\\\n    pub fn is_enabled(&amp;self) -&gt; bool {\\\\\\\\n        self.enabled\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    /// Track a generic event\\\\\\\\n    pub fn track_event(&amp;self, event_type: EventType, properties: serde_json::Value) {\\\\\\\\n        if !self.enabled {\\\\\\\\n            return;\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        let event = TelemetryEvent {\\\\\\\\n            timestamp: chrono::Utc::now().to_rfc3339(),\\\\\\\\n            event_type,\\\\\\\\n            version: env!(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"CARGO_PKG_VERSION\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\").to_string(),\\\\\\\\n            properties,\\\\\\\\n        };\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        if let Ok(json) = serde_json::to_string(&amp;event) {\\\\\\\\n            let _ = self.append_to_log(&amp;json);\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    /// Track command execution start\\\\\\\\n    pub fn track_command_start(&amp;mut self, command: &amp;str, args: &amp;[String]) {\\\\\\\\n        self.command_start = Some(Instant::now());\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        // Sanitize args - remove sensitive data\\\\\\\\n        let sanitized_args: Vec&lt;String&gt; = args\\\\\\\\n            .iter()\\\\\\\\n            .map(|arg| {\\\\\\\\n                // Hide potential sensitive values after certain flags\\\\\\\\n                if arg.starts_with(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"--private-key\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\n                    || arg.starts_with(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"--token\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\n                    || arg.starts_with(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"-p\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\n                {\\\\\\\\n                    format!(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"{}=***\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", arg.split('=').next().unwrap_or(arg))\\\\\\\\n                } else {\\\\\\\\n                    arg.clone()\\\\\\\\n                }\\\\\\\\n            })\\\\\\\\n            .collect();\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        self.track_event(\\\\\\\\n            EventType::CommandStarted,\\\\\\\\n            serde_json::json!({\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"session_id\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": &amp;self.session_id,\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"command\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": command,\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"args\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": sanitized_args,\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"arg_count\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": args.len(),\\\\\\\\n            }),\\\\\\\\n        );\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    /// Track command execution completion\\\\\\\\n    pub fn track_command_complete(&amp;self, command: &amp;str, success: bool, error: Option&lt;&amp;str&gt;) {\\\\\\\\n        let duration_ms = self\\\\\\\\n            .command_start\\\\\\\\n            .map(|start| start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64)\\\\\\\\n            .unwrap_or(0);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        self.track_event(\\\\\\\\n            EventType::CommandCompleted,\\\\\\\\n            serde_json::json!({\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"session_id\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": &amp;self.session_id,\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"command\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": command,\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"success\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": success,\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"duration_ms\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": duration_ms,\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"error\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": error.map(|e| e.to_string()),\\\\\\\\n            }),\\\\\\\\n        );\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    /// Track API call\\\\\\\\n    pub fn track_api_call(&amp;self, endpoint: &amp;str, method: &amp;str, status_code: u16, duration_ms: u64) {\\\\\\\\n        self.track_event(\\\\\\\\n            EventType::ApiCall,\\\\\\\\n            serde_json::json!({\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"session_id\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": &amp;self.session_id,\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"endpoint\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": endpoint,\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"method\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": method,\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"status_code\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": status_code,\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"duration_ms\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": duration_ms,\\\\\\\\n            }),\\\\\\\\n        );\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    /// Track error\\\\\\\\n    pub fn track_error(&amp;self, error_type: &amp;str, message: &amp;str, command: Option&lt;&amp;str&gt;) {\\\\\\\\n        self.track_event(\\\\\\\\n            EventType::Error,\\\\\\\\n            serde_json::json!({\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"session_id\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": &amp;self.session_id,\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"error_type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": error_type,\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"message\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": message,\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"command\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": command,\\\\\\\\n            }),\\\\\\\\n        );\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    /// Append line to log file\\\\\\\\n    fn append_to_log(&amp;self, line: &amp;str) -&gt; std::io::Result&lt;()&gt; {\\\\\\\\n        let mut file = OpenOptions::new()\\\\\\\\n            .create(true)\\\\\\\\n            .append(true)\\\\\\\\n            .open(&amp;self.log_path)?;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        writeln!(file, \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"{}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", line)?;\\\\\\\\n        Ok(())\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    /// Get telemetry log file path\\\\\\\\n    pub fn log_path(&amp;self) -&gt; &amp;PathBuf {\\\\\\\\n        &amp;self.log_path\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    /// Read all telemetry events (for debugging/analysis)\\\\\\\\n    pub fn read_events(&amp;self) -&gt; Vec&lt;TelemetryEvent&gt; {\\\\\\\\n        if !self.log_path.exists() {\\\\\\\\n            return vec![];\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        std::fs::read_to_string(&amp;self.log_path)\\\\\\\\n            .unwrap_or_default()\\\\\\\\n            .lines()\\\\\\\\n            .filter_map(|line| serde_json::from_str(line).ok())\\\\\\\\n            .collect()\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nimpl Drop for Telemetry {\\\\\\\\n    fn drop(&amp;mut self) {\\\\\\\\n        // Ensure any pending events are written\\\\\\\\n        if self.enabled &amp;&amp; self.command_start.is_some() {\\\\\\\\n            self.track_command_complete(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"unknown\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", false, Some(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"interrupted\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"));\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n#[cfg(test)]\\\\\\\\nmod tests {\\\\\\\\n    use super::*;\\\\\\\\n    use std::env;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    #[test]\\\\\\\\n    fn test_telemetry_disabled() {\\\\\\\\n        // Save original value\\\\\\\\n        let original = env::var(TELEMETRY_ENV_VAR).ok();\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        env::set_var(TELEMETRY_ENV_VAR, TELEMETRY_DISABLED);\\\\\\\\n        let telemetry = Telemetry::new();\\\\\\\\n        assert!(!telemetry.is_enabled());\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        // Restore original\\\\\\\\n        match original {\\\\\\\\n            Some(v) =&gt; env::set_var(TELEMETRY_ENV_VAR, v),\\\\\\\\n env::remove_var(TELEMETRY_ENV_VAR);\\\\\\\\n        let telemetry = Telemetry::new();\\\\\\\\n        // Note: This might fail if user has env var set\\\\\\\\n        // In real tests, use a temp directory\\\\\\\\n args - remove sensitive data\\\\\\\\n        let sanitized_args: Vec&lt;String&gt; = args\\\\\\\\n            .iter()\\\\\\\\n            .map(|arg| {\\\\\\\\n                // Hide potential sensitive values after certain flags\\\\\\\\n                if arg.starts_with(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"--private-key\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\n                    || arg.starts_with(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"--token\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\n                    || arg.starts_with(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"-p\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\n                {\\\\\\\\n                    format!(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"{}=***\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", arg.split('=').next().unwrap_or(arg))\\\\\\\\n                } else {\\\\\\\\n                    arg.clone()\\\\\\\\n                }\\\\\\\\n            })\\\\\\\\n            .collect();\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        self.track_event(\\\\\\\\n            EventType::CommandStarted,\\\\\\\\n            serde_json::json!({\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"session_id\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": &amp;self.session_id,\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"command\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": command,\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"args\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": sanitized_args,\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"arg_count\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": args.len(),\\\\\\\\n            }),\\\\\\\\n        );\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    /// Track command execution completion\\\\\\\\n    pub fn track_command_complete(&amp;self, command: &amp;str, success: bool, error: Option&lt;&amp;str&gt;) {\\\\\\\\n        let duration_ms = self\\\\\\\\n            .command_start\\\\\\\\n            .map(|start| start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64)\\\\\\\\n            .unwrap_or(0);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        self.track_event(\\\\\\\\n            EventType::CommandCompleted,\\\\\\\\n            serde_json::json!({\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"session_id\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": &amp;self.session_id,\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"command\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": command,\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"success\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": success,\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"duration_ms\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": duration_ms,\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"error\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": error.map(|e| e.to_string()),\\\\\\\\n            }),\\\\\\\\n        );\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    /// Track API call\\\\\\\\n    pub fn track_api_call(&amp;self, endpoint: &amp;str, method: &amp;str, status_code: u16, duration_ms: u64) {\\\\\\\\n        self.track_event(\\\\\\\\n            EventType::ApiCall,\\\\\\\\n            serde_json::json!({\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"session_id\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": &amp;self.session_id,\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"endpoint\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": endpoint,\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"method\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": method,\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"status_code\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": status_code,\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"duration_ms\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": duration_ms,\\\\\\\\n            }),\\\\\\\\n        );\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    /// Track error\\\\\\\\n    pub fn track_error(&amp;self, error_type: &amp;str, message: &amp;str, command: Option&lt;&amp;str&gt;) {\\\\\\\\n        self.track_event(\\\\\\\\n            EventType::Error,\\\\\\\\n            serde_json::json!({\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"session_id\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": &amp;self.session_id,\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"error_type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": error_type,\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"message\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": message,\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"command\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": command,\\\\\\\\n            }),\\\\\\\\n        );\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    /// Append line to log file\\\\\\\\n    fn append_to_log(&amp;self, line: &amp;str) -&gt; std::io::Result&lt;()&gt; {\\\\\\\\n        let mut file = OpenOptions::new()\\\\\\\\n            .create(true)\\\\\\\\n            .append(true)\\\\\\\\n            .open(&amp;self.log_path)?;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        writeln!(file, \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"{}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", line)?;\\\\\\\\n        Ok(())\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    /// Get telemetry log file path\\\\\\\\n    pub fn log_path(&amp;self) -&gt; &amp;PathBuf {\\\\\\\\n        &amp;self.log_path\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    /// Read all telemetry events (for debugging/analysis)\\\\\\\\n    pub fn read_events(&amp;self) -&gt; Vec&lt;TelemetryEvent&gt; {\\\\\\\\n        if !self.log_path.exists() {\\\\\\\\n            return vec![];\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        std::fs::read_to_string(&amp;self.log_path)\\\\\\\\n            .unwrap_or_default()\\\\\\\\n            .lines()\\\\\\\\n            .filter_map(|line| serde_json::from_str(line).ok())\\\\\\\\n            .collect()\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nimpl Drop for Telemetry {\\\\\\\\n    fn drop(&amp;mut self) {\\\\\\\\n        // Ensure any pending events are written\\\\\\\\n        if Some(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"interrupted\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"));\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n#[cfg(test)]\\\\\\\\nmod tests {\\\\\\\\n    use super::*;\\\\\\\\n    use Folders\\\":[],\\\"attachedFoldersNew\\\":[],\\\"bubbleId\\\":\\\"d89ab624-d1eb-4235-b186-3a40cd92924c\\\",\\\"userResponsesToSuggestedCodeBlocks\\\":[],\\\"suggestedCodeBlocks\\\":[],\\\"diffsForCompressingFiles\\\":[],\\\"relevantFiles\\\":[],\\\"toolResults\\\":[],\\\"notepads\\\":[],\\\"capabilities\\\":[],\\\"multiFileLinterErrors\\\":[],\\\"diffHistories\\\":[],\\\"recentLocationsHistory\\\":[],\\\"recentlyViewedFiles\\\":[],\\\"isAgentic\\\":false,\\\"fileDiffTrajectories\\\":[],\\\"existedSubsequentTerminalCommand\\\":false,\\\"existedPreviousTerminalCommand\\\":false,\\\"docsReferences\\\":[],\\\"webReferences\\\":[],\\\"aiWebSearchResults\\\":[],\\\"requestId\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"attachedFoldersListDirResults\\\":[],\\\"humanChanges\\\":[],\\\"attachedHumanChanges\\\":false,\\\"summarizedComposers\\\":[],\\\"cursorRules\\\":[],\\\"cursorCommands\\\":[],\\\"cursorCommandsExplicitlySet\\\":false,\\\"pastChats\\\":[],\\\"pastChatsExplicitlySet\\\":false,\\\"contextPieces\\\":[],\\\"editTrailContexts\\\":[],\\\"allThinkingBlocks\\\":[],\\\"diffsSinceLastApply\\\":[],\\\"deletedFiles\\\":[],\\\"supportedTools\\\":[],\\\"tokenCount\\\":{\\\"inputTokens\\\":0,\\\"outputTokens\\\":0},\\\"attachedFileCodeChunksMetadataOnly\\\":[],\\\"consoleLogs\\\":[],\\\"uiElementPicked\\\":[],\\\"isRefunded\\\":false,\\\"knowledgeItems\\\":[],\\\"documentationSelections\\\":[],\\\"externalLinks\\\":[],\\\"projectLayouts\\\":[],\\\"unifiedMode\\\":2,\\\"capabilityContexts\\\":[],\\\"todos\\\":[],\\\"createdAt\\\":\\\"2026-03-04T15:23:42.746Z\\\",\\\"mcpDescriptors\\\":[],\\\"workspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"conversationState\\\":{\\\"rootPromptMessagesJson\\\":[],\\\"turnsOld\\\":[],\\\"todos\\\":[],\\\"pendingToolCalls\\\":[],\\\"turns\\\":[],\\\"previousWorkspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"fileStates\\\":{},\\\"summaryArchives\\\":[],\\\"turnTimings\\\":[],\\\"fileStatesV2\\\":{},\\\"subagentStates\\\":{},\\\"selfSummaryCount\\\":0,\\\"readPaths\\\":[]},\\\"text\\\":\\\"\\\\n# StandX-CLI Codebase Architecture Summary\\\\n\\\\n## 1. Project Structure\\\\n\\\\n### Directory Layout\\\\n\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\nstandx-cli/\\\\n├── src/                    # Main source code\\\\n│   ├── main.rs             # Entry point, splash screen, command dispatch\\\\n│   ├── lib.rs              # Library root, public API\\\\n│   ├── cli.rs              # CLI argument parsing (clap)\\\\n│   ├── commands.rs         # Command handlers\\\\n│   ├── config.rs           # Configuration management\\\\n│   ├── error.rs            # Error types\\\\n│   ├── models.rs           # Data models\\\\n│   ├── output.rs           # Output formatting (table, JSON, CSV)\\\\n│   ├── telemetry.rs        # Local usage telemetry\\\\n│   ├── websocket.rs        # WebSocket client for streaming\\\\n│   ├── auth/\\\\n│   │   ├── mod.rs          # StandXSigner, RequestSignature\\\\n│   │   └── credentials.rs  # JWT + private key storage\\\\n│   └── client/\\\\n│       ├── mod.rs          # StandXClient (HTTP), public API\\\\n│       ├── account.rs      # Account/positions/orders API\\\\n│       └── order.rs        # Order creation/cancellation\\\\n├── tests/                  # Unit, integration, E2E tests\\\\n├── docs/                   # User documentation (12 markdown files)\\\\n├── openclaw/               # OpenClaw skill metadata\\\\n├── clawhub-publish/        # ClawHub publish config\\\\n├── homebrew/               # Homebrew formula\\\\n├── scripts/                # Utility scripts\\\\n├── Cargo.toml\\\\n└── API_DOCUMENTATION.md\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## 2. CLI Argument Parsing &amp; Commands\\\\n\\\\n### Entry Point (&#96;src/main.rs&#96;)\\\\n\\\\n- Parses args with &#96;Cli::parse()&#96; (clap)\\\\n- Shows splash screen when no args or &#96;--help&#96;/&#96;-h&#96;\\\\n- Sets logging from &#96;--verbose&#96; / &#96;-q&#96; / &#96;--quiet&#96;\\\\n- Supports &#96;--dry-run&#96;, &#96;--openclaw&#96; (JSON output), &#96;--yes&#96;\\\\n- Calls &#96;execute_command()&#96; for the chosen command\\\\n\\\\n### CLI Definition (&#96;src/cli.rs&#96;)\\\\n\\\\n**Root struct &#96;Cli&#96;:**\\\\n\\\\n- &#96;command: Commands&#96; (subcommand)\\\\n- Global flags: &#96;--config&#96;, &#96;--output&#96; (table|json|csv|quiet), &#96;--verbose&#96;, &#96;--quiet&#96;, &#96;--openclaw&#96;, &#96;--dry-run&#96;, &#96;--yes&#96;\\\\n\\\\n**Commands enum:**\\\\n\\\\n| Command   | Subcommands / Args | Purpose |\\\\n|----------|---------------------|---------|\\\\n| &#96;Config&#96; | Init, Set, Get, Show | Config management |\\\\n| &#96;Auth&#96;   | Login, Logout, Status | Auth management |\\\\n| &#96;Market&#96; | Symbols, Ticker, Tickers, Trades, Depth, Kline, Funding | Market data |\\\\n| &#96;Account&#96;| Balances, Positions, Orders, History, Config | Account data |\\\\n| &#96;Order&#96;  | Create, Cancel, CancelAll | Order management |\\\\n| &#96;Trade&#96;  | History | Trade history |\\\\n| &#96;Leverage&#96; | Get, Set | Leverage |\\\\n| &#96;Margin&#96; | Transfer, Mode | Margin |\\\\n| &#96;Stream&#96; | Price, Depth, Trade, Order, Position, Balance, Fills | WebSocket streams |\\\\n| &#96;Dashboard&#96; | Snapshot (symbols, verbose, watch, compact) | Unified dashboard |\\\\n| &#96;Portfolio&#96; | Snapshot (verbose, watch) | Portfolio summary |\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## 3. Command Dispatch &amp; Execution\\\\n\\\\n### Flow (&#96;src/main.rs&#96; → &#96;src/commands.rs&#96;)\\\\n\\\\n1. &#96;main()&#96; parses &#96;Cli&#96; and calls &#96;execute_command(cli.command, output, verbose)&#96;.\\\\n2. &#96;execute_command()&#96; matches on &#96;Commands&#96; and calls the right handler:\\\\n\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;rust\\\\nmatch command {\\\\n    Commands::Config { command } =&gt; commands::handle_config(command, output).await?,\\\\n    Commands::Auth { command } =&gt; commands::handle_auth(command).await?,\\\\n    Commands::Market { command } =&gt; commands::handle_market(command, output).await?,\\\\n    Commands::Account { command } =&gt; commands::handle_account(command, output).await?,\\\\n    Commands::Order { command } =&gt; commands::handle_order(command).await?,\\\\n    Commands::Trade { command } =&gt; commands::handle_trade(command, output).await?,\\\\n    Commands::Leverage { command } =&gt; commands::handle_leverage(command, output).await?,\\\\n    Commands::Margin { command } =&gt; commands::handle_margin(command).await?,\\\\n    Commands::Stream { command } =&gt; commands::handle_stream(command, verbose).await?,\\\\n    Commands::Dashboard { ... } =&gt; commands::handle_dashboard(command, output).await?,\\\\n    Commands::Portfolio { ... } =&gt; commands::handle_portfolio(command, output).await?,\\\\n}\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\n\\\\n3. Each handler uses &#96;StandXClient&#96; for API calls and &#96;output::format_*&#96; for display.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## 4. API Client Layer\\\\n\\\\n### HTTP Client (&#96;src/client/mod.rs&#96;)\\\\n\\\\n- **Base URL:** &#96;https://perps.standx.com&#96;\\\\n- **HTTP client:** &#96;reqwest&#96; with rustls, 30s timeout\\\\n- **Auth:** &#96;build_auth_headers()&#96;:\\\\n  - JWT in &#96;Authorization: Bearer &lt;token&gt;&#96;\\\\n  - Ed25519 signing for trading (optional private key)\\\\n  - Headers: &#96;x-request-sign-version&#96;, &#96;x-request-id&#96;, &#96;x-request-timestamp&#96;, &#96;x-request-signature&#96;\\\\n\\\\n**Public endpoints (no auth):**\\\\n\\\\n- &#96;GET /api/query_symbol_info&#96; → &#96;Vec&lt;SymbolInfo&gt;&#96;\\\\n- &#96;GET /api/query_symbol_market?symbol=&#96; → &#96;MarketData&#96;\\\\n- &#96;GET /api/query_symbol_price?symbol=&#96; → &#96;PriceData&#96;\\\\n- &#96;GET /api/query_recent_trades?symbol=&amp;limit=&#96; → &#96;Vec&lt;Trade&gt;&#96;\\\\n- &#96;GET /api/query_depth_book?symbol=&amp;limit=&#96; → &#96;OrderBook&#96;\\\\n- &#96;GET /api/kline/history?symbol=&amp;resolution=&amp;from=&amp;to=&#96; → &#96;Vec&lt;Kline&gt;&#96;\\\\n- &#96;GET /api/query_funding_rates?symbol=&amp;start_time=&amp;end_time=&#96; → &#96;Vec&lt;FundingRate&gt;&#96;\\\\n\\\\n### Account Client (&#96;src/client/account.rs&#96;)\\\\n\\\\n- Uses &#96;auth_headers()&#96; (JWT only)\\\\n- Endpoints: &#96;query_balance&#96;, &#96;query_positions&#96;, &#96;query_open_orders&#96;, &#96;query_orders&#96;, &#96;query_trades&#96;, &#96;query_position_config&#96;, &#96;change_leverage&#96;, &#96;change_margin_mode&#96;, &#96;transfer_margin&#96;\\\\n\\\\n### Order Client (&#96;src/client/order.rs&#96;)\\\\n\\\\n- Uses &#96;build_auth_headers(payload)&#96; (JWT + Ed25519 signing)\\\\n- Endpoints: &#96;POST /api/new_order&#96;, &#96;POST /api/cancel_order&#96;, &#96;POST /api/cancel_orders&#96;\\\\n\\\\n### WebSocket (&#96;src/websocket.rs&#96;)\\\\n\\\\n- **URL:** &#96;wss://perps.standx.com/ws-stream/v1&#96;\\\\n- Public: &#96;StandXWebSocket::without_auth()&#96; for price, depth, public trades\\\\n- Authenticated: &#96;StandXWebSocket::new()&#96; for order, position, balance, fills\\\\n- Message types: &#96;Price&#96;, &#96;Depth&#96;, &#96;Trade&#96;, &#96;Position&#96;, &#96;Balance&#96;, &#96;Order&#96;, etc.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## 5. Dashboard-Related Code\\\\n\\\\n### Dashboard Command (&#96;src/commands.rs&#96;)\\\\n\\\\n- **Handler:** &#96;handle_dashboard()&#96; → &#96;fetch_and_display_dashboard()&#96;\\\\n- **Data:** balance, positions, orders, market tickers, recent trades\\\\n- **Symbol filter:** optional comma-separated symbols\\\\n- **Watch mode:** &#96;--watch N&#96; refreshes every N seconds (Ctrl+C to stop)\\\\n- **Compact mode:** &#96;--compact&#96; hides RECENT TRADES\\\\n- **Auth:** works without auth (market only); shows warning if not authenticated\\\\n\\\\n### Dashboard Output (&#96;src/output.rs&#96;)\\\\n\\\\n- &#96;format_dashboard_mvp()&#96;: ASCII box layout with:\\\\n  - TICKERS (symbol, mark price, change %)\\\\n  - ACCOUNT (balance, available, PnL)\\\\n  - POSITIONS (symbol, side, entry, mark, PnL)\\\\n  - ORDER BOOK (first symbol)\\\\n  - ACTIVE ORDERS\\\\n  - RECENT TRADES (unless compact)\\\\n\\\\n### Portfolio Command\\\\n\\\\n- **Handler:** &#96;handle_portfolio()&#96; → &#96;fetch_and_display_portfolio()&#96;\\\\n- **Data:** &#96;PortfolioSnapshot&#96; (total value, PnL 24h, unrealized PnL, positions)\\\\n- **Watch mode:** same as dashboard\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## 6. Cargo.toml Dependencies\\\\n\\\\n| Category | Crate | Purpose |\\\\n|----------|-------|---------|\\\\n| CLI | clap 4.5 | Argument parsing |\\\\n| Async | tokio 1.43, futures, futures-util | Async runtime |\\\\n| HTTP | reqwest 0.12 (rustls) | REST client |\\\\n| WebSocket | tokio-tungstenite 0.26 | WebSocket |\\\\n| Serialization | serde, serde_json, toml | JSON/TOML |\\\\n| Errors | anyhow, thiserror | Error handling |\\\\n| Config | config 0.15, dirs 6.0 | Config and paths |\\\\n| Output | tabled 0.18, comfy-table 7.1, csv 1.3 | Tables and CSV |\\\\n| Logging | tracing, tracing-subscriber | Logging |\\\\n| Crypto | ed25519-dalek 2.1, bs58 0.5, rand 0.8 | Ed25519 signing |\\\\n| Security | keyring 3.0 (optional) | Credential storage |\\\\n| Time | chrono 0.4 | Timestamps |\\\\n| Utils | hex, base64, once_cell, rpassword, regex, uuid | Helpers |\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## 7. Key Data Types &amp; Models\\\\n\\\\n### Market (&#96;src/models.rs&#96;)\\\\n\\\\n- &#96;SymbolInfo&#96; – symbol metadata (decimals, fees, leverage)\\\\n- &#96;MarketData&#96; – mark/index/last price, volume, funding\\\\n- &#96;PriceData&#96; – price snapshot\\\\n- &#96;OrderBook&#96; – bids/asks &#96;Vec&lt;[String; 2]&gt;&#96;\\\\n- &#96;Trade&#96; – public trade\\\\n- &#96;Kline&#96; – OHLCV\\\\n- &#96;FundingRate&#96; – funding rate history\\\\n\\\\n### Trading\\\\n\\\\n- &#96;OrderSide&#96; – Buy, Sell\\\\n- &#96;OrderType&#96; – Limit, Market\\\\n- &#96;TimeInForce&#96; – Gtc, Ioc, Fok\\\\n- &#96;OrderStatus&#96; – New, PartiallyFilled, Filled, Canceled, etc.\\\\n- &#96;Order&#96; – order details\\\\n- &#96;Position&#96; – position details\\\\n- &#96;PositionConfig&#96; – leverage config\\\\n\\\\n### Account\\\\n\\\\n- &#96;Balance&#96; – balance, equity, PnL\\\\n- &#96;DashboardSnapshot&#96; – account, positions, orders, market, trades\\\\n- &#96;PortfolioSnapshot&#96; – total value, PnL, positions\\\\n\\\\n### Auth\\\\n\\\\n- &#96;Credentials&#96; – JWT, optional Ed25519 private key\\\\n- &#96;StandXSigner&#96; – Ed25519 signing\\\\n- &#96;RequestSignature&#96; – version, request_id, timestamp, signature\\\\n\\\\n### Error (&#96;src/error.rs&#96;)\\\\n\\\\n- &#96;Error&#96; enum: Http, WebSocket, Json, Api, AuthRequired, InvalidCredentials, TokenExpired, InvalidSymbol, RateLimitExceeded, Config, Io, Validation, DryRun, Unknown\\\\n- &#96;to_json()&#96;, &#96;is_retryable()&#96;, &#96;suggested_action()&#96;\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Important Files Quick Reference\\\\n\\\\n| File | Purpose |\\\\n|------|---------|\\\\n| &#96;src/main.rs&#96; | Entry point, splash, dispatch |\\\\n| &#96;src/cli.rs&#96; | Clap CLI definitions |\\\\n| &#96;src/commands.rs&#96; | Command handlers |\\\\n| &#96;src/client/mod.rs&#96; | HTTP client, public API |\\\\n| &#96;src/client/account.rs&#96; | Account/positions/orders API |\\\\n| &#96;src/client/order.rs&#96; | Order create/cancel |\\\\n| &#96;src/models.rs&#96; | Data models |\\\\n| &#96;src/auth/credentials.rs&#96; | JWT + key storage |\\\\n| &#96;src/auth/mod.rs&#96; | Ed25519 signing |\\\\n| &#96;src/output.rs&#96; | Table/JSON/CSV, dashboard layout |\\\\n| &#96;src/websocket.rs&#96; | WebSocket client |\\\\n| &#96;src/config.rs&#96; | Config load/save |\\\\n| &#96;src/error.rs&#96; | Error types |\\\\n| &#96;API_DOCUMENTATION.md&#96; | API reference <think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I have a thorough understanding of the codebase. Let me analyze `commands.rs` and `output.rs` for the dashboard MVP (Issue #156) and provide a comprehensive review.

Let me identify the key issues:

### commands.rs Issues:

1. **Sequential API calls in `fetch_and_display_dashboard`**: All API calls (balance, positions, orders, market data per symbol, trades) are made sequentially. For N symbols, that's N+4 sequential HTTP requests. This is very slow. Should use `tokio::join!` or `futures::join_all` for parallelism.

2. **`StandXClient::new()` called inside `fetch_and_display_dashboard`**: A new client is created every refresh cycle. 

3. **Tickers fetched one-by-one**: The `market` data for each symbol is fetched in a sequential loop, which should be parallelized instead.

4. **When no symbol filter is specified, it fetches ALL symbols**: First calls `get_symbol_info()` to get all symbols, then fetches market data for each one individually. This can be extremely slow with many sequential requests.

5. **Missing order book data**: The DashboardSnapshot doesn't include order book data (which is in Issue #156's spec). The ORDER BOOK section in `format_dashboard_mvp` only displays placeholder information.

6. **Watch mode doesn't flush output**: After clearing the screen, there's no explicit flush call, which might cause display issues.

7. **CLI flag naming inconsistency**: Issue #156 specifies `--symbol` (singular) but the current implementation uses `--symbols` (plural, comma-separated).

8. **Error handling in watch mode**: If fetching fails during the watch loop, the entire loop breaks instead of catching the error and continuing to display updates.

9. **Unnecessary enum wrapper**: The `DashboardCommands` enum only has one variant, making it redundant since the CLI already handles the routing.

10. **Unused verbose parameter**: The verbose flag gets passed through but never actually used for any output.

11. **Fragile auth error detection**: Checking error messages for "401" or "Unauthorized" strings is brittle and should use typed errors instead.

12. **Ctrl+C responsiveness issue**: The signal handler checks the flag after sleeping, so the worst-case response time is the full interval duration.

13. **Duplicated watch logic**: Both dashboard and portfolio commands have nearly identical watch mode implementations that should be consolidated.

Now looking at the output formatting issues — the hardcoded width of 65 characters doesn't adapt to terminal size and several box-drawing lines are missing their right borders, breaking the visual structure in multiple places like the positions, order book, and active orders sections.

The ticker change calculation is also incorrect since it's computing distance from the 24-hour low rather than actual price change, which would display misleading data to users. Additionally, the ORDER BOOK section lacks actual bid/ask data because the snapshot doesn't include that information, and the `Tabled` implementations use a dummy `LENGTH = 100` value that doesn't reflect the actual field counts.

The `format_dashboard_mvp` function doesn't handle long strings that exceed the box width, causing overflow issues. The header formatting is also misaligned—it left-aligns the refresh time when the spec calls for right-alignment. While the dashboard uses directional arrows for positive/negative values, there's no color differentiation, and the usage hint line shown in the spec is completely missing. I should review the full analysis and create a comprehensive plan to address these issues systematically.</details></think>

{
  "plan": "# Dashboard MVP Code Review (Issue #156)\n\n## Critical Bugs\n\n### 1. Ticker change percentage calculation is wrong\n\nIn &#96;format_dashboard_mvp&#96;, the price change is computed as &#96;(last - low_24h) / low_24h&#96; -- this measures distance from 24h low, NOT actual 24h price change. This will always show a positive percentage and mislead users.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;219:266:src/output.rs\nlet last: f64 = m.last_price.parse().unwrap_or(0.0);\nlet low: f64 = m.low_24h.parse().unwrap_or(0.0);\nlet change = if low &gt; 0.0 {\n    ((last - low) / low) * 100.0\n} else {\n    0.0\n};\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**Fix:** Use a proper 24h change field from &#96;MarketData&#96; (if available), or compute from &#96;open_24h&#96; price. If neither exists, fetch it from the API or note it as unsupported.\n\n### 2. Broken box-drawing borders -- multiple lines missing the right &#96;│&#96;\n\nThese lines lack the closing border character, breaking the visual layout:\n\n- Line 298: &#96;\"│ POSITIONS:\\n\"&#96; -- no &#96;│&#96; on right\n- Line 300: &#96;\"│   No open positions\\n\"&#96; -- no &#96;│&#96; on right\n- Line 325: &#96;\"│ ORDER BOOK:\\n\"&#96; -- no &#96;│&#96; on right\n- Line 336: &#96;\"│ ACTIVE ORDERS:\\n\"&#96; -- no &#96;│&#96; on right\n- Line 338: &#96;\"│   No open orders\\n\"&#96; -- no &#96;│&#96; on right\n- Line 358: &#96;\"│   No recent trades\\n\"&#96; -- no &#96;│&#96; on right\n\nThe other lines that DO have &#96;│&#96; use &#96;{:&lt;width$}&#96; padding, but these inconsistent lines break the entire box aesthetic.\n\n### 3. Missing ORDER BOOK data in DashboardSnapshot\n\n&#96;DashboardSnapshot&#96; has no &#96;OrderBook&#96; field. The ORDER BOOK section only prints the symbol name with no actual bid/ask levels. Issue #156 requires displaying ask/bid prices and quantities.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;325:333:src/output.rs\noutput.push_str(\"│ ORDER BOOK:\\n\");\nif let Some(m) = snapshot.market.first() {\n    output.push_str(&amp;format!(\n        \"│   Symbol: {:&lt;width$} │\\n\",\n        m.symbol,\n        width = width - 14\n    ));\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**Fix:** Add &#96;depth: Option&lt;OrderBook&gt;&#96; to &#96;DashboardSnapshot&#96;, fetch it in &#96;fetch_and_display_dashboard&#96;, and render top N bid/ask levels.\n\n---\n\n## Performance Issues\n\n### 4. All API calls are sequential -- extremely slow for multiple symbols\n\nIn [&#96;fetch_and_display_dashboard&#96;](src/commands.rs), each call blocks the next:\n1. &#96;get_symbol_info()&#96; (when no filter)\n2. &#96;get_balance()&#96;\n3. &#96;get_positions()&#96;\n4. &#96;get_open_orders()&#96;\n5. &#96;get_symbol_market()&#96; -- **in a sequential loop per symbol**\n6. &#96;get_recent_trades()&#96;\n\nFor 10 symbols, this is 14+ sequential HTTP requests. With typical latency of 100-300ms per request, a single refresh can take 2-5 seconds.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;974:980:src/commands.rs\nlet mut market = Vec::new();\nfor symbol in &amp;symbol_list {\n    if let Ok(ticker) = client.get_symbol_market(symbol).await {\n        market.push(ticker);\n    }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**Fix:** Use &#96;tokio::join!&#96; for independent calls (balance, positions, orders in parallel), and &#96;futures::future::join_all&#96; for the per-symbol market data loop.\n\n### 5. &#96;StandXClient::new()&#96; is recreated on every watch refresh\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;894:src/commands.rs\nlet client = StandXClient::new()?;\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;fetch_and_display_dashboard&#96; is called every N seconds in watch mode, each time constructing a new HTTP client with fresh connection pools. This wastes connection reuse.\n\n**Fix:** Create the client once in &#96;handle_dashboard&#96; and pass it by reference.\n\n### 6. No-filter mode fetches ALL symbols then queries each individually\n\nWhen &#96;--symbols&#96; is not provided, it calls &#96;get_symbol_info()&#96; to list all symbols, then loops over every single one to call &#96;get_symbol_market()&#96;. If the exchange has 50 symbols, that is 50 sequential HTTP calls for tickers alone.\n\n**Fix:** Consider adding a batch ticker endpoint (like the existing &#96;MarketCommands::Tickers&#96; pattern), or at minimum parallelize the calls. Alternatively, limit to a sensible default set.\n\n---\n\n## Design / UX Issues\n\n### 7. Dashboard width hardcoded to 65 characters\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;222:src/output.rs\nlet width = 65;\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThis doesn't adapt to terminal size. Long symbol names, large numbers, or multiple tickers easily overflow the fixed width, causing misaligned borders.\n\n**Fix:** Either detect terminal width with &#96;terminal_size&#96; crate, or use a wider default (e.g., 78-80), and truncate/wrap content that overflows.\n\n### 8. Header layout doesn't match Issue #156 spec\n\nThe spec shows:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n│  standx dashboard                                       refresh: 10:32 │\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nBut the current code produces:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n│ standx dashboard refresh: 10:32                                        │\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nTitle and timestamp should be on opposite ends (left-aligned title, right-aligned time).\n\n### 9. Error in watch mode kills the entire loop\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;862:877:src/commands.rs\nloop {\n    // ...\n    fetch_and_display_dashboard(&amp;symbol_list, verbose, output_format, compact)\n        .await?;\n    tokio::time::sleep(...).await;\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe &#96;?&#96; operator propagates any transient API error (network timeout, rate limit) and exits watch mode completely. Users would have to restart the command.\n\n**Fix:** Catch errors, display them in the dashboard, and continue the next iteration.\n\n### 10. &#96;--symbol&#96; (singular) vs &#96;--symbols&#96; (plural) mismatch with spec\n\nIssue #156 specifies &#96;--symbol BTCUSDT&#96; (singular). The implementation uses &#96;--symbols&#96; (plural, comma-separated). This is a minor CLI ergonomics issue -- consider supporting both with a &#96;visible_alias&#96;.\n\n### 11. &#96;verbose&#96; flag is accepted but never used\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;888:893:src/commands.rs\nasync fn fetch_and_display_dashboard(\n    symbol_filter: &amp;[String],\n    _verbose: bool,\n    ...\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe &#96;_verbose&#96; parameter is prefixed with underscore (intentionally unused). Users who pass &#96;--verbose&#96; see no additional output.\n\n### 12. Watch mode Ctrl+C response delay\n\nThe Ctrl+C flag is checked only at the top of the loop. If the dashboard just started a sleep of N seconds, the user must wait up to N seconds before the program exits.\n\n**Fix:** Use &#96;tokio::select!&#96; between the sleep and a signal future for immediate response.\n\n---\n\n## Code Quality Issues\n\n### 13. &#96;DashboardCommands&#96; enum has a single variant -- unnecessary indirection\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;338:354:src/cli.rs\npub enum DashboardCommands {\n    Snapshot { ... }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;Commands::Dashboard&#96; already has the fields inline. The &#96;DashboardCommands::Snapshot&#96; wrapper is created synthetically in &#96;main.rs&#96; just to be matched away in &#96;commands.rs&#96;. This adds no value.\n\n**Fix:** Remove &#96;DashboardCommands&#96;, handle &#96;Commands::Dashboard&#96; fields directly.\n\n### 14. Duplicate watch-mode boilerplate between dashboard and portfolio\n\n&#96;handle_dashboard&#96; and &#96;handle_portfolio&#96; share nearly identical Ctrl+C setup, clear-screen, loop, and sleep logic. This should be factored into a reusable &#96;run_watch_loop&#96; helper.\n\n### 15. Auth error detection by string matching is fragile\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;918:930:src/commands.rs\nif err_str.contains(\"401\")\n    || err_str.contains(\"Unauthorized\")\n    || err_str.contains(\"Authentication required\")\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThis can break if error message format changes. Use the typed &#96;Error&#96; variants (&#96;AuthRequired&#96;, &#96;TokenExpired&#96;) from &#96;src/error.rs&#96; instead.\n\n### 16. &#96;Tabled&#96; impl &#96;LENGTH&#96; constants are incorrect\n\nMultiple &#96;Tabled&#96; implementations set &#96;const LENGTH: usize = 100&#96;, which doesn't match the actual field count. While &#96;tabled&#96; may not strictly enforce this, it's semantically wrong and could cause issues with future versions.\n\n---\n\n## Missing Features per Issue #156\n\n| Feature | Status |\n|---------|--------|\n| Static output | Implemented |\n| &#96;--watch N&#96; auto-refresh | Implemented |\n| &#96;--symbol&#96; filtering | Implemented (as &#96;--symbols&#96;) |\n| &#96;--compact&#96; mode | Implemented |\n| &#96;-o json&#96; output | Implemented |\n| ORDER BOOK with bid/ask data | **Missing** -- only shows symbol name |\n| Correct price change % | **Broken** -- uses wrong formula |\n| Usage hint in footer | **Missing** |\n\n---\n\n## Summary of Recommended Actions (Priority Order)\n\n1. **Fix ticker change % calculation** (bug -- shows wrong data)\n2. **Add OrderBook data to DashboardSnapshot** and render it (missing feature)\n3. **Fix broken box borders** (visual bug)\n4. **Parallelize API calls with &#96;tokio::join!&#96;** (performance -- critical for watch mode)\n5. **Handle errors gracefully in watch mode** (resilience)\n6. **Fix header layout** (match Issue #156 spec)\n7. **Adapt dashboard width to terminal** (UX)\n8. **Reuse &#96;StandXClient&#96; across watch iterations** (performance)\n9. **Use &#96;tokio::select!&#96; for responsive Ctrl+C** (UX)\n10. **Extract shared watch-mode logic** (code quality)\n",
